sub_topics.title: Community Practices
competences: 1.1.13.1 Use appropriate language to talk about community practices

instructional_blocks.hook: Good community practices
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrases community and practices
Explain that community practices is when people work together to help everyone
Use a poster or chart showing people working together (sweeping, sharing, planting trees)
Ask learners to mention good community practices
Demonstrate: Teacher: What are they doing? Learner: They are sweeping
Supervise groups to ensure everyone practices
Ask some groups to present their dialogue in front of class
Provide feedback
Ask learners to mention good community practices
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Learner 1: What are they doing? Learner 2: They are planting trees
Present dialogue in front of class

instructional_blocks.hook: How we live together in harmony
teacher_steps: Introduce the word harmony
Explain that harmony is when people work together to make something better
Display a poster showing people living in harmony (kind, sharing, caring, honest)
Demonstrate: Teacher: What is he/she doing? Learner: He/she is being caring
Rotate pairs
Put learners into groups for role-play demonstrating harmony
Have each group present to class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Learner 1: What is he/she doing? Learner 2: He/she is sharing
In groups, role-play living in harmony

instructional_blocks.hook: Keeping our community clean
teacher_steps: Revise the word community
Explain that a community is a group of people who live, work and help each other
Ask learners to mention how they keep the community clean
Demonstrate: Teacher: How do we keep the community clean? Learner: By slashing
Pair learners and rotate
Go to school ground and demonstrate one way to keep community clean
learner_steps: Mention how they keep community clean
Practice in pairs: How do we keep our community clean? By putting litter in the bin
Go to school ground and demonstrate (picking rubbish, slashing, sweeping, weeding)

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on equipping learners with the ability to use appropriate language to talk about community practices. Understanding community practices builds unity, promotes respect and a sense of responsibility to self, family and community. Community practices include sweeping, sharing, planting trees. Living in harmony involves being kind, fair, honest, and caring. Keeping the community clean involves slashing, sweeping, picking rubbish, and weeding.

vocabulary.definition: A group of people who live, work and help each other
Activities that people do regularly as part of community life
When people work together to make something better

questions: Ask learners to mention some good community practices
Ask learners to mention how people live together in harmony
Ask learners to mention ways of keeping the community clean

sub_topics.title: Statements
competences: 1.1.14.1 Use appropriate language to deny, confirm, correct and express statements and propositions

instructional_blocks.hook: Denying statements
teacher_steps: Introduce the word deny and explain what it means
Demonstrate: Teacher: Is this your pen? Learner: No, it is not my pen
Conduct whole-class choral repetition of denial phrases
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Ask learners to explain what it means to deny
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Learner 1: Is this your book? Learner 2: No it is not my book
Learner 2: Are you a boy? Learner 1: No I am not a boy
Showcase dialogue in front of class

instructional_blocks.hook: Confirming statements
teacher_steps: Revise denying
Introduce the word confirm and explain
Demonstrate: Teacher: Is this your bag? Learner: Yes, it is my bag
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Play ball game: throw a ball and ask learners to deny or confirm
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Is this your crayon? Yes it is my crayon

instructional_blocks.hook: Asking and answering questions - denying and confirming
teacher_steps: Revise denying and confirming
Introduce the phrase to correct and explain
Engage learners in group activity
Put learners in pairs
Instruct pairs to showcase
learner_steps: Participate in group activity: Group A: What are you doing? Group B: We are learning
Group A: You are walking. Group B: No, we are learning

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on using various statements in conversations. Denying means saying something is not true (e.g., No, it is not my pen). Confirming means agreeing that something is true (e.g., Yes, it is my bag). Correcting means fixing a wrong statement made by another person.

vocabulary.definition: To say that something is not true or does not belong to you
To say that something is true or to agree with a statement
To fix or change a wrong statement to make it accurate

questions: Ask learners to explain what it means to deny
Ask learners to explain the meaning of confirm
Ask learners to correct statements

sub_topics.title: Plurals
competences: 1.1.15.1 Use the plural forms of regular nouns

instructional_blocks.hook: Changing regular nouns from singular to plural form - adding /s/ sound
teacher_steps: Introduce the word plural
Explain that plural is when things are more than one
Explain that regular nouns change to plural by adding /s/ sound
Display chart showing singular and plural
Ask learners to name things in singular then plural (book-books, cup-cups, pot-pots, blanket-blankets)
Demonstrate: What is this? This is a cat. What are these? These are cats
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Ask each pair to perform
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Name things in singular then plural
Practice in pairs: What is this? This is a book. What are these? These are books
Perform dialogue in front of class

instructional_blocks.hook: Changing regular nouns from singular to plural form - adding /z/ sound
teacher_steps: Revise /s/ plurals
Explain some nouns change to plural by adding /z/ sound
Ask learners to name things in plural using /z/ sound (bag-bags, girl-girls, dog-dogs, pencil-pencils)
Demonstrate: What is this? This is a bag. What are these? These are bags
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners and rotate
Ask each pair to perform
learner_steps: Name things in plural using /z/ sound
Practice in pairs: What is this? This is a pencil. What are these? These are pencils
Perform dialogue in front of class

instructional_blocks.hook: Changing regular nouns from singular to plural form - adding /e/ and /z/ sound
teacher_steps: Recap /z/ plurals
Display chart showing nouns taking /e/ and /z/ sounds
Ask learners to name in plural (dish-dishes, box-boxes, bus-buses, dress-dresses, class-classes)
Demonstrate: What is this? This is a box. What are these? These are boxes
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners and rotate
Ask each pair to perform
learner_steps: Name things in plural using /e/ and /z/ sounds
Practice in pairs: What is this? This is a bus. What are these? These are buses
Perform dialogue in front of class

sub_topics.short_notes: This sub-topic focuses on changing regular nouns from singular to plural form. Three plural formation rules: (1) adding /s/ sound: book-books, cup-cups, pot-pots, blanket-blankets, cat-cats, stick-sticks; (2) adding /z/ sound: bag-bags, girl-girls, dog-dogs, pencil-pencils; (3) adding /e/ and /z/ sound for nouns ending in s, sh, ch, x: dish-dishes, box-boxes, bus-buses, dress-dresses, class-classes.

vocabulary.definition: When things are more than one
When referring to just one thing
A noun that follows standard rules to form its plural

questions: Ask learners to name items in singular and plural form
Ask learners to mention regular nouns in singular and plural form

sub_topics.title: Manner
competences: 1.2.1.1 Use appropriate language to talk about good manners and behavior

instructional_blocks.hook: Table manners
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrase table manners
Explain table manners as the way you conduct yourself while eating
Display a poster showing a family having a meal
Ask learners to say what they see (praying, washing hands, not talking with food in mouth)
Demonstrate: What are good table manners? Praying before eating
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups role-play good table manners
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Observe the poster and mention what they see
Practice in pairs: What are good table manners? Washing hands before eating
Role-play good table manners in groups

instructional_blocks.hook: Being kind
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrase being kind
Explain being kind as caring, helping and respecting others
Show a picture of someone helping another person
Ask learners how they show kindness (sharing food, sharing pencils, helping elderly)
Demonstrate: How can we show kindness? Sharing food
Pair learners to practice
Groups role-play kindness
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: How can we show kindness? Sharing pencils

instructional_blocks.hook: Respecting personal space
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrase personal space
Explain what respecting personal space means
Give examples: can I come in, can I sit next to you, can I pass
Demonstrate: Excuse me, can I come in? Yes you can
Conduct choral repetition of Excuse me, can I_____?
Pair learners to practice
Ask each pair to perform dialogue
learner_steps: Listen to explanation of personal space
Practice in pairs: Excuse me, can I come in? Yes you can
Excuse me, can I sit next to you? Yes you can
Excuse me, can I talk to you? Yes you can
Perform dialogue in front of class

sub_topics.short_notes: In Term 3, Etiquette focuses on manners. Good table manners include praying before eating, washing hands before eating, not talking with food in the mouth, not playing while eating, and helping clean the table. Being kind involves sharing food, sharing pencils, and helping elderly people. Respecting personal space means asking permission using phrases like Excuse me, can I...?

vocabulary.definition: To treat someone with care and consideration
Telling the truth and being trustworthy
Being on time
Saying sorry for something wrong you have done
Being thankful
The polite ways of behaving towards other people
The area around a person that should be respected by others

questions: Ask learners to mention good table manners
Ask learners to mention ways of showing kindness
Ask learners to mention ways of respecting personal space

sub_topics.title: Simple Stories

instructional_blocks.hook: Listening to simple stories
teacher_steps: Ask learners to mention stories they know
Tell learners a simple story at their level (folktale, fable)
Demonstrate: Teacher: Tell a simple story. Learners: Listen to a simple story
Put learners in pairs
Rotate pairs
Groups: one learner tells while others listen
Ask each group to present
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Tell a simple story / Listen to a simple story
In groups, one learner tells while others listen

instructional_blocks.hook: Retelling simple stories
teacher_steps: Ask learners to say stories they know
Tell a simple story
Ask learners to retell it
Demonstrate: Teacher: Tell a simple story. Learners: Retell the simple story
Put learners in pairs to retell
Rotate pairs
Groups: one learner retells while others listen
Provide feedback
Randomly pick learners to retell
learner_steps: In groups, one learner retells while others listen
Present retelling in front of class

instructional_blocks.hook: Answering oral questions based on the text
teacher_steps: Display picture/chart showing characters in a story
Tell a simple story
Ask learners to mention the characters
Demonstrate: Teacher: Who/What is in the story? Learner: Mother/The lion
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice answering questions
Rotate pairs
Ask each pair to perform
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Who/What ate the food? Bibusa/Kalulu ate the food
Perform dialogue in front of class

sub_topics.short_notes: In Term 3, stories focuses on simple stories. Learners listen to and retell simple stories and answer oral questions. Story types include: folktales, fables, traditional stories, fairytales, and decodable stories. This builds communication skills, confidence, and fosters social relationships.

vocabulary.definition: Traditional stories passed down through generations
Short stories with a moral lesson, usually featuring animals
Stories told for many years in a particular culture
Stories featuring magical creatures and events
Stories written with words that follow regular phonics patterns

questions: Ask learner to listen to a simple story
Ask learners to retell the simple story
Ask learners oral questions based on the story

sub_topics.title: Simple Songs
competences: 1.4.1.1 Appropriate language in simple songs used correctly

instructional_blocks.hook: Listening to and singing simple songs - The Alphabet Song
teacher_steps: Recap on songs from previous lesson
Display a poster showing the alphabet
Introduce the alphabet song
Explain the importance of the alphabet song
Demonstrate: Teacher: Sing the alphabet song. Learner: Listen to the alphabet song
Conduct whole-class choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups sing the alphabet song
Ask each group to perform
Provide feedback
Ask learners to sing as a class
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Sing the alphabet song / Listen to the alphabet song
In groups, sing the alphabet song

instructional_blocks.hook: Listening to and singing simple songs - The Greeting Song
teacher_steps: Recap on songs from previous lesson
Display a poster showing greetings
Introduce the greeting song
Demonstrate: Teacher: Sing the greeting song. Learner: Listen to the greeting song
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups sing the greeting song
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Sing the greeting song / Listen to the greeting song
In groups, sing the greeting song

instructional_blocks.hook: Answering oral questions based on the song - alphabet and greeting
teacher_steps: Recap on songs from previous lesson
Display chart showing alphabet/greetings
Explain the lesson/message/moral from the songs
Demonstrate: Why do we sing the alphabet song? To know the letters of the alphabet
Why do we sing the greeting song? To know how to greet
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Ask each pair to perform
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Why do we sing the alphabet song? To know the letters of the alphabet
Why do we sing the greeting song? To know how to greet
Perform dialogue in front of class

instructional_blocks.hook: Listening to and singing simple songs - The Number Song
teacher_steps: Recap on songs from previous lesson
Display a poster showing numbers
Introduce the number song
Demonstrate: Teacher: Sing the number song. Learner: Listen to the number song
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups sing the number song
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Sing the number song / Listen to the number song
In groups, sing the number song

instructional_blocks.hook: Listening to and singing simple songs - Days of the Week Song
teacher_steps: Recap on songs from previous lesson
Display poster showing days of the week
Introduce the days of the week song
Demonstrate: Teacher: Sing the days of the week song. Learner: Listen to the days of the week song
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups sing the days of the week song
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Sing the days of the week song / Listen to the days of the week song
In groups, sing the days of the week song

instructional_blocks.hook: Answering oral questions based on the song - number and days of the week
teacher_steps: Recap on songs from previous lesson
Display chart showing numbers/days of the week
Explain the lesson/message/moral
Demonstrate: Why do we sing the number song? To know how to count numbers
Why do we sing the days of the week song? To know days of the week
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Ask each pair to perform
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: Why do we sing the number song? To know how to count numbers
Why do we sing the days of the week song? To know days of the week
Perform dialogue in front of class

sub_topics.short_notes: In Term 3, songs is a topic developing vocabulary, correct pronunciation, and communication skills through rhythm and melody. Songs foster respect, build relationships, and promote social harmony. Key songs: greeting song, days of the week song, alphabet song, number song, months song, national anthem.

vocabulary.definition: A song used to say hello and welcome others
A song that helps learners remember the seven days of the week
A song that helps learners remember the letters of the alphabet
A song that helps learners learn how to count numbers
An official song representing a nation

questions: Ask learners to sing the alphabet song
Ask learners to sing the greeting song
Ask learners to answer oral questions based on the song
Ask learners to sing the number song
Ask learners to sing the days of the week song

sub_topics.title: Role Playing
competences: 1.5.1.1 Use appropriate language to depict real life situations

instructional_blocks.hook: Events
teacher_steps: Introduce the word events and explain what it is
Ask learners to mention events they have attended
Display a poster showing different events (birthdays, Christmas, independence, sports day, wedding)
Ask learners to mention events shown on the chart
Demonstrate: Teacher: What event is this/that? Learner: It is Christmas
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups role-play one event
Ask each group to perform
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: What event is this/that? It is a wedding / It is a birthday party

instructional_blocks.hook: Simple events - Christmas
teacher_steps: Recap on events
Display chart showing what happens on Christmas
Demonstrate: What do people do on Christmas? They go to church
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups role-play Christmas activities
Provide feedback
Ask learners to sing a Christmas song
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: What do people do on Christmas? They dance / They eat nice food

instructional_blocks.hook: Simple events - Birthdays
teacher_steps: Recap on events
Display chart showing what happens on birthdays
Demonstrate: What do people do on birthdays? They eat cake
Conduct choral repetition
Pair learners to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups role-play birthday activities
Provide feedback
Ask learners to sing a birthday song
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: What do people do on birthdays? They sing / They dance

sub_topics.short_notes: In Term 3, drama focuses on role-playing. Drama provides a dynamic, engaging and authentic environment for language practice, fostering fluency, confidence and real world communication skills. Events covered: birthdays (eating cake, singing, dancing), Christmas (going to church, dancing, eating nice food), Independence Day, sports day, wedding, and baptism.

vocabulary.definition: Special occasions or celebrations that people participate in
Celebrations held each year on the day someone was born
A Christian holiday celebrated on 25th December
A national holiday celebrating a country's freedom
A day when people participate in athletic events and competitions
A ceremony where two people get married
A religious ceremony welcoming someone into the church

questions: Ask learners to mention different events
Ask learners to mention things that people do on Christmas
Ask learners to mention things that people do on birthdays

sub_topics.title: Simple Poems

instructional_blocks.hook: Reciting simple poems on animals - Baa Baa Black Sheep
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrase poem
Explain that a poem is using words in a style to talk about something
Display a picture showing a black sheep
Recite a simple poem loudly with fun (baa-baa black sheep...)
Demonstrate: Recite a simple poem on animals (baa baa black sheep...). Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups recite poem on animals in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Observe the picture of a black sheep
Practice in pairs: take turns reciting and listening
In groups, recite the poem on animals

instructional_blocks.hook: Reciting simple poems on colors - Red Red Like an Apple Ripe
teacher_steps: Arrange desks with pictures/real objects of different colors
Tell learners to identify the colors
Recite a simple poem on colors (red, red like an apple ripe...)
Demonstrate: Recite a simple poem on colors. Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups recite poem on colors in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Listen to the poem on colors
Practice in pairs: take turns reciting and listening
In groups, recite the poem on colors

instructional_blocks.hook: Reciting simple poems on family - A Mother's Touch So Warm
teacher_steps: Ask learners to mention members of the family
Display a picture showing father, mother and children
Recite a simple poem on family (a mother's touch, so warm...)
Demonstrate: Recite a simple poem on family. Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups recite poem on family in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Practice in pairs: take turns reciting and listening
In groups, recite the poem on family

instructional_blocks.hook: Repeating rhymes - Twinkle Twinkle Little Star
teacher_steps: Introduce the word rhyme
Display a picture showing things with rhyming words (dog, frog, fog, log)
Explain that rhymes are words that sound the same at the end
Repeat the rhyme (Twinkle, twinkle little star...)
Demonstrate: Repeat the rhyme. Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups repeat the rhyme in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Listen and repeat Twinkle Twinkle Little Star
Practice in pairs: take turns repeating and listening

instructional_blocks.hook: Repeating rhymes - The Little Cat (cat, hat, rat, bat, fat)
teacher_steps: Revise the word rhyme
Ask learners to mention rhyming words (cat, hat, rat, bat, fat)
Display chart showing a cat, hat, rat and bat
Demonstrate: Repeat the rhyme (the little cat, so big and fat, sat upon a purple hat...). Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups repeat the rhyme in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Mention rhyming words (cat, hat, rat, bat, fat)
Practice in pairs: take turns repeating and listening

instructional_blocks.hook: Repeating rhymes - Hickory Dickory Dock
teacher_steps: Revise the word rhyme
Ask learners to mention rhyming words (cook, book, took, look, hook)
Demonstrate: Repeat the rhyme (Hickory dickory dock, the mouse ran up...). Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Play rhyme game: word cards in box, rhyming words around classroom, learners find matching words
Ask learners to identify rhyming words from the repeated rhyme
learner_steps: Mention rhyming words (cook, book, took, look, hook)
Listen and repeat Hickory Dickory Dock
Practice in pairs: take turns repeating and listening
Participate in rhyme game: find rhyming words around classroom
Identify rhyming words from the repeated rhyme

sub_topics.short_notes: In Term 3, poetry focuses on simple poems. Learners recite poems on animals (baa-baa black sheep), colors (red, red like an apple ripe), and family (a mother's touch, so warm). Rhymes are words that sound the same at the end. Rhyme groups: dog/frog/fog/log; cat/hat/rat/bat/fat; cook/book/took/look/hook. Nursery rhymes: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star, The Little Cat poem, Hickory Dickory Dock.

vocabulary.definition: Using words in a style to talk about something, often with rhythm
Words that sound the same at the end e.g. cat, hat, bat
To say something aloud from memory
Living creatures such as sheep, dogs, cats
Visual properties of objects such as red, blue, yellow
A group of people related to each other
A relationship between people who care about and support each other

questions: Ask learners to recite the simple poem on animals
Ask learners to recite the simple poem on colors
Ask learners to recite the simple poem on family
Ask learners to repeat a number of rhymes

sub_topics.title: Tongue Twisters

instructional_blocks.hook: Reciting tongue twisters - Red lorry yellow lorry
teacher_steps: Introduce the phrase tongue twisters
Display a picture showing the red lorry and yellow lorry
Recite the tongue twister (red lorry, yellow lorry)
Explain that tongue twisters are sentences that twist your tongue when said quickly
Demonstrate: Recite the tongue twisters (red lorry, yellow lorry). Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups recite the tongue twister in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Listen to explanation of tongue twisters
Listen and repeat (red lorry, yellow lorry)
Practice in pairs: take turns reciting and listening
In groups, recite the tongue twister

instructional_blocks.hook: Reciting tongue twisters - Peter Piper
teacher_steps: Revise tongue twisters
Recite Peter piper picked a perk of pickled peppers
Demonstrate: Recite the tongue twisters (Peter piper...). Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups recite the tongue twister in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Listen and repeat Peter Piper tongue twister
Practice in pairs: take turns reciting and listening
In groups, recite the tongue twister

instructional_blocks.hook: Reciting tongue twisters - A flea and a fly
teacher_steps: Revise tongue twisters
Recite A flea and a fly flew up in a flue
Say it fast three times
Demonstrate: Recite the tongue twisters (A flea and a fly...). Learners listen
Conduct whole class repetition
Put learners in pairs to practice
Rotate pairs
Groups recite the tongue twister in front of class
Provide feedback
learner_steps: Listen and repeat A flea and a fly flew up in a flue
Practice in pairs: take turns reciting and listening
In groups, recite the tongue twister

sub_topics.short_notes: Tongue twisters are sentences that twist your tongue when said quickly. They improve pronunciation and phonological awareness. Three tongue twisters: (1) Red lorry, yellow lorry - focuses on /r/ and /l/ sounds; (2) Peter piper picked a perk of pickled peppers - focuses on /p/ sounds; (3) A flea and a fly flew up in a flue - focuses on /fl/ consonant clusters.

vocabulary.definition: A sentence that is difficult to say quickly because of similar sounds
The way sounds are made when speaking words

questions: Ask learners to recite the given tongue twisters
Ask learners to recite red lorry, yellow lorry
Ask learners to recite Peter piper picked a perk of pickled peppers
Ask learners to recite A flea and a fly flew up in a flue
